result.vlm_summary:
page_state:
Google Flights results page for one-way Sydney → San Francisco on Tue, Mar 10. “Best” tab selected; top flights list visible.
task_relevant_visible_text:
“One way”, “Sydney”, “San Francisco”, “Tue, Mar 10”, “Top flights”, “Sorted by top flights”, “1 stop”, “Nonstop”, “18 hr 20 min”, “13 hr 30 min”, “13 hr 45 min”, prices $620, $756, $757.
selected_candidate:
Air New Zealand 11:45 AM – 12:05 PM, 1 stop, 18 hr 20 min, $620.
constraint_evidence:
- one-way flight: supported (“One way” visible)
- origin Sydney: supported (“Sydney” visible)
- destination San Francisco: supported (“San Francisco” visible)
- departing on March 10, 2026: supported by URL context and visible date “Tue, Mar 10”
- shortest layover: not_visible (no layover duration shown for any option; only stop count and total duration visible)
- flight result selection/booking not yet made: supported by page state (results list only)
action_target:
Send exit message to user as instructed: “[EXIT]”
success_signals:
User-facing exit message sent; no further browser interaction needed.
failure_signals:
Attempting to infer or select a flight based on layover without visible layover data; navigating away instead of exiting.
uncertainty:
The screenshot does not show layover durations, so the “shortest layover” criterion cannot be evaluated from visible evidence.

task_context.task: navigate: Search for one-way flights from Sydney to San Francisco, departing on March 10, 2026, with the shortest layover.
task_context.title: Find Cheap Flights Worldwide & Book Your Ticket - Google Flights

observation.title: Find Cheap Flights Worldwide & Book Your Ticket - Google Flights

args.query: Google Flights task with results page visible but layover duration not visible; shortest layover constraint cannot be verified

items.summary: The agent completed a search task but did not enforce an explicit user constraint before stopping.
items.fix_hint: Track hard constraints separately from the main goal and verify them against the final page state before returning success.

items.summary: The agent exited before reaching a page state that satisfies the task.
items.fix_hint: Require a final evidence check that the requested information or state is visible before sending an exit message.

args.expected_behavior: Before exiting, verify the 'shortest layover' constraint on the flight results and ensure the selected candidate or final state satisfies all requested qualifiers, or otherwise continue interacting to surface the needed layover information.
args.actual_behavior: The run reached a Google Flights results page showing one-way Sydney → San Francisco on Mar 10, 2026, but the visible evidence only showed stop count and total duration; layover duration was not visible. The agent then sent an exit message without verifying the shortest-layover requirement.
args.regression_rule: Do not mark a flight-search task complete until the final page explicitly supports every hard constraint, including layover-specific qualifiers; if a qualifier is not visible, keep exploring or open candidate details instead of exiting.

evidence.claim: Final page is a Google Flights results page for one-way Sydney → San Francisco on Tue, Mar 10 with top flights listed.
Visible text includes 'One way', 'Sydney', 'San Francisco', 'Tue, Mar 10', and flight options with durations and prices.
Layover duration was not visible, only stop count and total duration; therefore the 'shortest layover' condition was not verified.
The agent exited immediately after reaching results, without further checking candidate details or layover information.
The run contains no concrete evidence that a shortest-layover flight was selected or validated.

suggested_followups.label: Why missed constraint?
suggested_followups.message: Show how the results page lacked layover-duration evidence and why that makes the exit premature.

suggested_followups.label: Compare with success
suggested_followups.message: Find a similar successful Google Flights run that explicitly verified the layover constraint before exiting.
